terms.definition: The identity P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B) that reverses conditional probability, valid when P(B) > 0.
P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B)/P(B): the probability of A among outcomes where B has occurred, requiring P(B) > 0.
P(A): the probability of hypothesis A before observing evidence B; the starting belief in a Bayes update.
P(B|A): the probability (or density) of observing evidence B when hypothesis A is true; not a probability distribution over A.
P(A|B): the updated probability of A after observing evidence B, computed via Bayes' theorem.
P(B): the overall probability of observing B, acting as the normalization constant in Bayes' theorem; often computed by the law of total probability.
For a partition {A₁,…,Aₙ}, P(B) = Σᵢ P(B|Aᵢ)P(Aᵢ); used to compute the Bayes denominator from pieces already known.
